turned on $4,000 acres in April.
1909. The work included the con-
struction of large power plant at
Priest Rapids, on the Columbia
river, where $4,000 horsepower is
available; pumping plant at Coy
ote Rapids, power canal 1925
feet Ion, a wing dam 5000 feet
long: an irrigation canal nearly 20
miles long, with the necessary lat
erals, and other work at an ex-
peRse of approximately $1,000, 900.
A Second ditch. which will put
$4,000 acres under irrigation, will
be completed next year and opened
about the same time as the Tieton
and Okanogan bitches, according
to the present estimate of those in
charge of the government projects.
Thus single private corporation
will put under actual irrigation in
three years larger acreage than
is contemplated in the two large
government projects, to be accom-
plished in five years, and at half
the estimated expense of the gov
eminent projects.


In addition, the private projects
will have dependable electrical
power to lift the water of the Col
umbia river on their lands, while
the government projects depend on
gravity flow, subject to the short
age of the low water stages of the
Yakima river
